description_long: This is a classic wooden barrel with a cylindrical shape, slightly bulging at the center and tapering towards the top and bottom. The exterior is constructed from vertical wooden staves, each displaying a natural wood grain texture and some stylized carved markings. Two dark metal hoops encircle the barrel, one near the top and one near the bottom, providing structural reinforcement. The top surface is flat and features a visible wooden bung or stopper set into the center, suggesting the barrel can be sealed. The wood appears well-worn, with subtle nicks and stylized details, giving it a rustic, hand-crafted appearance. This type of barrel is commonly found in urban environments such as alleys, storage areas, or near taverns and markets.
description: A rustic wooden barrel with metal hoops, wood grain texture, and a central bung on top. The barrel is slightly bulged and reinforced, suitable for storage or decorative urban use.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wood grain, two metal hoops, and a visible bung on the top surface.
description_view_1: Side view with a smoother wood panel, less pronounced markings, and the bung still visible on top.
description_view_2: Rear view with a wide, plain wood panel and the bung offset to the left on the top.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with more carved markings and both metal hoops clearly visible.
